differentials.name: Arterial obstruction: thromboembolism, plaque thrombus [Peripheral vasculopathy]
Venous obstruction: thrombosis, bowel torsion, closed-loop obstruction
differentials.raw: Venous obstruction: thrombosis, bowel torsion, closed-loop obstruction

differentials.name: Hypoperfusion: proximal arterial stenosis potentiated by myocardial infarction, bradycardia, dehydration N.B.:   closed-loop obstruction with signs of bowel infarction is a surgical condition!
differentials.raw: Hypoperfusion: proximal arterial stenosis potentiated by myocardial infarction, bradycardia, dehydration N.B.:   closed-loop obstruction with signs of bowel infarction is a surgical condition!

context: √  signs of bowel infarction: √  free peritoneal fluid √  asymmetric bowel wall enhancement √  persistent enhancement of bowel wall / segmental arteries √  arterial / venous filling defects √  increased density of mesentery √  bowel obstruction
